decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry duration is 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails must be rate-limited
decisions.detail: Password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting and require implementation.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Max 3 password reset attempts per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.
Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: API gateway technology
decisions.detail: The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: API gateway rate limit per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will impose a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.

evidence.quote: with a 100 req/min limit per user.

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by Auth API spec
decisions.detail: The mobile app team cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Target publish date for Auth API spec
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the Auth API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints
decisions.detail: All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests

decisions.title: Automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off
decisions.detail: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway fails
decisions.detail: The question of what happens if the API gateway becomes unavailable needs to be answered.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: It is an open question whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user's password is reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate-limiting for password reset email requests.
Enforce a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour.
Develop and publish the Authentication API specification.
Develop automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry duration must be set to 24 hours.
The API gateway must impose a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05 to unblock the mobile app team.
Automated integration tests for authentication endpoints are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated when a user's password is reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully authenticates and receives a JWT, When 23 hours and 59 minutes pass since the token was issued, Then the JWT token must still be valid for authentication.
Given a user successfully authenticates and receives a JWT, When 24 hours and 1 minute pass since the token was issued, Then the JWT token must be expired and invalid for authentication.
Given a user attempts to reset their password, When they have initiated fewer than 3 password reset requests within the last hour, Then a password reset email must be successfully sent to the user.

Given a user attempts to reset their password, When they have initiated 3 or more password reset requests within the last hour, Then no password reset email must be sent to the user, and the system must provide an appropriate message indicating the rate limit has been reached.
Given an authenticated user sends requests to the API, When the user sends 100 or fewer requests within a 60-second period, Then all requests must be processed successfully by the API gateway.

Given an authenticated user sends requests to the API, When the user sends more than 100 requests within a 60-second period, Then all subsequent requests exceeding 100 within that 60-second period must be rejected with a '429 Too Many Requests' HTTP status code (or equivalent).
Automated integration tests must be implemented for the 'login' authentication endpoint.
Automated integration tests must be implemented for the 'refresh token' authentication endpoint.
Automated integration tests must be implemented for the 'password reset' authentication endpoint.
All automated integration tests for the authentication endpoints must pass before Sprint 3 sign-off.
test_scenarios: Successful login: Verify a valid JWT is returned and can be used immediately.
Near JWT expiry check: Use a valid JWT to make an API call at 23 hours 59 minutes after issuance.
Post-JWT expiry check: Attempt to use the same JWT to make an API call at 24 hours 1 minute after issuance, expect rejection.
Password reset successful request: Request a password reset for a valid user. Verify email is sent.
Password reset boundary success: Request password reset 3 times within a 30-minute window for the same user. Verify 3 emails are sent.

Password reset rate limit trigger: Request password reset a 4th time within the same 30-minute window for the same user. Verify no email is sent and an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Too many reset attempts, please try again later') is displayed.
Password reset rate limit reset: Wait 61 minutes after the 4th failed attempt, then request another password reset successfully for the same user. Verify email is sent.
API Gateway rate limit within limit (99 requests): Send 99 requests to a user-authenticated endpoint within a 50-second window. Verify all succeed.
API Gateway rate limit at limit (100 requests): Send 100 requests to a user-authenticated endpoint within a 50-second window. Verify all succeed.
API Gateway rate limit exceed limit (101st request): Send 101 requests to a user-authenticated endpoint within a 50-second window. Verify the 101st request receives a 429 status code.
API Gateway rate limit recovery: After receiving a 429, wait 61 seconds and then send another request. Verify it succeeds.
Auth API integration tests existence: Confirm the presence and execution of automated integration tests for login, refresh, and password reset endpoints.
Auth API integration tests passing: Verify that these tests pass as part of the CI/CD pipeline or before Sprint 3 sign-off.
edge_cases: JWT Token: Attempt to use a valid token from a significantly different system clock time (e.g., client clock is off).
JWT Token: Token validation when network latency causes requests to arrive just at or after the 24-hour mark.
Password Reset: Attempt multiple password resets for a non-existent user. Verify no email is sent and a generic message is displayed (to prevent user enumeration).
Password Reset: Attempt password resets for the same user from different IP addresses within an hour. (Expected: Should still hit the limit for the user).
Password Reset: Attempt password resets for different users from the same IP address within an hour. (Expected: Should not block other users).
Password Reset: Rapid-fire requests for password reset (e.g., 5 requests in 1 second).
API Gateway Rate Limiting: Concurrent requests from a single user that cross the rate limit threshold (e.g., 50 requests, then 51 more concurrently).
API Gateway Rate Limiting: Requests from different authenticated users sharing the same public IP address (if applicable to 'per user' definition).
API Gateway Rate Limiting: Verify that API Gateway rate limiting applies to all authentication endpoints as well as other user-authenticated API endpoints.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable? (Ownership: PM/QA for requirements definition, SA for system-level spec)
Are refresh tokens invalidated when a user's password is reset? If so, what is the expected user experience for existing sessions? (Ownership: PM/QA)

decision_history: The JWT token expiry duration is set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2, confirming existing policy.
Password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting and require implementation to enhance security.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway as the chosen technology.
The API gateway will impose a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.
Development and QA teams are dependent on clarity regarding refresh token invalidation on password reset for implementation and testing (inferred).
Engineering team requires a defined fallback behavior for the API gateway to ensure robust system design and implementation (inferred).
risks: Delay in publishing the Auth API specification (target 2026-07-05) could block the mobile app team, impacting their overall delivery timeline (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).
Security Vulnerability: If refresh tokens are not appropriately invalidated upon password reset, it could lead to security compromises (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).

Service Outage Risk: The lack of a defined fallback mechanism if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable could lead to significant service disruption and impact system availability (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).
open_questions: What is the defined fallback behaviour if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable? (Impact: High - affects system resilience and service continuity. Ownership: PM to facilitate decision, SA/Engineering for technical solution.)
Are refresh tokens invalidated automatically when a user's password is reset? (Impact: High - critical for security and user experience. Ownership: PM/QA to define requirement.)
milestones: Complete automated integration tests for authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) before Sprint 3 sign-off.

system_requirements: The system SHALL ensure JWT tokens expire after 24 hours (D001).
The system SHALL implement rate-limiting for password reset email requests (D002).
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour per user (D003).
The API Gateway SHALL impose a limit of 100 requests per minute per user (D005).
The system SHALL include automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints (D008).
data_flows: User requests flow through the AWS API Gateway to backend authentication services (inferred from D004, D005).
Authentication service sends password reset emails via an email delivery service (inferred from D002, D003).
Authentication service issues and validates JWT tokens with a 24-hour expiry (inferred from D001).
Authentication service exposes 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' endpoints to client applications (inferred from D008).
interface_contracts: An Authentication API specification (Auth API spec) MUST be published (D006).
The Auth API specification MUST define the endpoints for login, refresh, and reset functionality (inferred from D008).
The Auth API specification SHALL detail the JWT token structure and handling, including its 24-hour expiry (inferred from D001).
The Auth API specification SHALL define the contract for password reset functionality, including its rate-limiting mechanism of 3 attempts per hour (inferred from D002, D003).

constraints.text: The Authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05 (D007).
Automated integration tests for authentication endpoints MUST be completed before Sprint 3 sign-off (D009).
Development for the mobile application is blocked until the Authentication API specification is published (D006).

architectural_decisions.text: The JWT token expiry duration is set to 24 hours (D001).
Password reset attempts are limited to a maximum of 3 per hour (D003).
The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway (D004).

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior or resiliency strategy if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable? (D010)
Is the refresh token invalidated when a user's password is reset? If so, what is the specific mechanism? (D011)

stakeholder.business_summary: This briefing covers key decisions made regarding our system's security, user authentication, and API development. Important steps are being taken to protect user accounts and ensure system stability. A critical dependency for the mobile app team's progress has also been identified and scheduled.
key_impact: User accounts will be better protected from malicious attacks due to new limits on password reset attempts and overall system usage.
The mobile app development timeline is directly impacted; work is blocked until a key technical specification for user authentication is finalized and published by July 5th.
The reliability and quality of our user login and password reset functions will improve significantly with new automated testing requirements, ensuring a smoother user experience.
Our system will have enhanced stability and performance due to the implementation of strict limits on how many requests can be made at one time.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan for continuing service if the main system that handles all user requests (API gateway) experiences an outage?
For security, when a user changes their password, should we automatically log them out of all active sessions across all devices?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, quality issues, or contradictions were found. The outputs are well-structured and tailored to their respective audiences. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content detected.
